title: Event Camera (DVS) — intensity reconstruction from asynchronous events
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in canonical event-based vision literature (Lichtsteiner-Posch-Delbruck 2008 DVS, Rebecq E2VID, Scheerlinck FireNet).

E.description: Each pixel of an event sensor (DVS / DAVIS) independently emits an event e = (t, u, v, p) with polarity p = +/-1 whenever the log-intensity change since the last event at that pixel crosses a contrast threshold C. The asynchronous event stream encodes temporal brightness changes at microsecond resolution but not absolute intensity. Reconstructing a standard intensity video I(u,v,t) from the event stream is an ill-posed temporal inverse problem.
E.forward_model: emit e_k = (t_k, u_k, v_k, p_k)  iff  |log(I(u,v,t_k)) - log(I(u,v,t_k^last))| >= C
E.world_state_x: Dense intensity video I(u,v,t) in R^{H x W x N_t}, non-negative
E.observation_y: Asynchronous event stream E = {e_k = (t_k, u_k, v_k, p_k)}, k = 1..N_events
physical_parameters_theta: Contrast threshold C (typ. 0.15-0.3 in log units) with per-pixel variance sigma_C
Refractory period tau_r (microseconds) limiting event rate per pixel
Bias currents controlling contrast / bandwidth trade-off
Optics (lens, aperture) and sensor resolution H x W

W.regime: Intensity reconstruction is ill-posed (DC level is unobservable; only log-differentials are sampled). Unique reconstruction requires an anchor frame (DAVIS hybrid output) or a strong prior (video smoothness, learned models). Contrast-threshold variance sigma_C and noise events add strong instability at low event rates.

C.solver_class: E2VID (recurrent neural net), FireNet (U-Net), EV-Gait (Poisson-Events), event-to-frame reconstruction with optical-flow prior
C.error_bound: ||I_hat - I||_2 <= C1 * sigma_C + C2 / sqrt(event_rate) + C3 * anchor_interval; higher motion -> more events -> better reconstruction
C.complexity: O(N_events) for async integration; O(H * W * N_t) for recurrent reconstruction
